subcodes.title: Words to pilgrims in June 1906
subcodes.first_line: Bahá'u'lláh has said that whoever has an art or handicraft, it is incumbent upon him to endeavor to develop it to the point of perfection - even though that art be as humble as that of weaving coarse straw matting.
subcodes.subjects: @heaven and paradise; heaven and hell, @perfection; imperfection of nature, @progress only takes place within its own degree, @excellence; distinction, @work as worship
